name: Sparkfed Surgebot
flavour_text: They said it was too dangerous to activate. They were right.
tips: This card hard-counters sacrifice strategies. If your opponent scores a sacrifice and your board is empty, react with Surgebot for a free 27/25 to immediately recover.
The no-allies gate means this works best as a comeback card. If your board has been wiped, an opponent sacrifice lets you instantly recover with a premium body for 0 mana.
In Robot decks, RGB Lasercannon's discard cost can thin your Robot count. If Surgebot is your last card and your board is empty, you're set up for the react.
Don't be afraid to play this normally for 5 mana if the react condition seems unlikely — 27/25 melee is strong raw stats for the cost.
Against Rat decks, Giant Rat's death-promote is not a sacrifice and won't trigger the react. Surgebot shines when the opponent sacrifices their last minion and you have nothing left on board.
rulings: Can be played normally as a 5-mana minion, or played as a free react card (0 mana) when the opponent sacrifices a minion.
The react mode can only be used if you have no friendly minions on the board. If you already control any minion, the react option is unavailable.
When played via react, you choose any empty cell in your back 2 rows to place Sparkfed Surgebot — same placement rules as playing it normally.
If your opponent sacrifices and you have no minions, you may react with this card even if you could not afford its normal 5-mana cost.
